title: Rachael Rollins, former U.S. Attorney who resigned amid ethics scandal, reportedly running again for Suffolk DA
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rachael Rollins, former U.S. Attorney, filed to run for Suffolk County District Attorney on Wednesday.
Rollins previously served as Suffolk DA from 2019 to 2021 before resigning as U.S. Attorney amid ethics scandal in 2023.
Current DA Kevin Hayden and attorney Linda Champion are also candidates for the Suffolk DA position.

title: Firefighters ask for support for colleague critically injured in training academy fall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Chelmsford firefighter Nick Spinale, 28, critically injured in 40-foot fall at Massachusetts Firefighting Academy.
Spinale suffers facial fractures, severe internal organ damage, and wrist fractures; requires multiple surgeries.
Community and firefighting unions rally support for Spinale, treated in UMass Memorial ICU in Worcester.

title: How an old Roman Anthony TikTok video inspired Red Sox’ new celebration
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox introduced a new celebration inspired by a TikTok dance featuring outfielder Roman Anthony.
Manager Alex Cora confirmed the celebration origins from Anthony's high school video dancing to Mariah Carey's 'Obsessed.'
Roman Anthony is batting .217 in 12 games this season, with Boston holding a 4-8 record after a win against Milwaukee.

title: Former Mass. police chief charged with committing fraud while leading department
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former Adams Police Chief Kevin Scott Kelley charged with fraud, including false overtime claims totaling nearly $20,000.
Kelley's alleged fraudulent activities occurred during work under a traffic enforcement grant from January 2024 to September 2025.
Adams Select Board placed Kelley on paid leave in September 2025; his arraignment is set for May 4, 2026.

title: North Andover police unions call for ‘immediate removal’ of chief after Fitzsimmons acquittal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Andover police unions passed a vote of no confidence in Chief Charles Gray, calling for his immediate removal.
Unions cited poor leadership, lack of body cameras, and low morale as reasons for Chief Gray's removal.
Kelsey Fitzsimmons, acquitted of assault, plans to file a civil lawsuit against town officials post-acquittal.

title: Report: Celtics are ‘known to be interested’ in trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Celtics are reportedly considered potential suitors for trading Giannis Antetokounmpo as his situation unfolds with the Bucks.
Antetokounmpo's current contract runs through 2028, with a chance the Bucks may assess his trade value due to ongoing struggles.
Financially, Celtics face challenges, with Antetokounmpo's high salary complicating a potential pairing with Tatum and Brown under the salary cap.

title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox pull away from Brewers, 5-0, to earn first series win
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Sonny Gray led the Red Sox to a 5-0 victory over the Brewers, improving his ERA to 2.76.
Tyler Samaniego struck out all three batters he faced in his major league debut for Boston.
The Red Sox walked eight times against Milwaukee's pitching, capitalizing on opportunities to win their first series of the season.

title: Wu releases $4.9 billion budget proposal amid ‘difficult moment’ for city’s finances
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu proposed a $4.9 billion budget for Boston's 2027 fiscal year, marking a 2.1% spending increase.
The budget faces challenges due to rising health insurance costs, police overtime, and snow removal expenses.
A $1.7 billion budget for Boston Public Schools was approved, aligning staff levels with declining student enrollment.

title: Rob Gronkowski joins Logan Mankins, Adam Vinatieri on Patriots Hall of Fame ballot
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Rob Gronkowski, Logan Mankins, and Adam Vinatieri are finalists for the 2026 Patriots Hall of Fame class.
Fans will decide which player is inducted into the Patriots Hall of Fame, with results announced after April 28.
Gronkowski is a first-time finalist, while Mankins and Vinatieri have been finalists multiple times before.

title: Bruins sign top prospect James Hagens: How can he help Boston’s playoff push?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins sign 19-year-old James Hagens to a three-year entry-level contract to bolster playoff roster.
Hagens, drafted seventh overall, played six games with Providence, scoring one goal and three assists.
Boston expects Hagens's skills to enhance third-line play and power-play unit amid playoff push.
